mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent within a single month, it signals labor market stabilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly positive when UNRATE crosses below 7.0% (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in consumer prices and increases in retail sales indicate disinflationary growth, compressing term premiums and lifting equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint momentum of CPIAUCSL and RSAFS shows no predictive power for 60d forward returns (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of high inflation signals supply-side resilience that supports corporate earnings, generating positive 20d returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to produce positive 20d returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects housing sector recovery, which leads consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth rates show no correlation with 5d forward SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Employment increases exceeding 150,000 in a single month signal robust job creation, reducing recession risk and pushing SPX higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll surprises above 150k do not yield statistically significant positive 20d forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling policy rates and rising industrial output indicates accommodative policy supporting real output, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive 60d returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and INDPRO fails to explain variance in 60d forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized signals strong aggregate demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive SPX returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to produce positive 5d forward returns in walk-forward testing.
